engineIntelligence.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the N74B66TU twin-turbo V12 engine fitted to all Rolls Royce Ghost Series II models in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II N74B66TU engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo oil feed line coking - carbon buildup restricts oil flow to turbos, causing premature failure at 60k-90k miles.
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases with blue smoke on high-mileage units, typically after 70k miles.
VANOS solenoid failure - variable valve timing faults cause rough idle, power loss, and fault codes on N74B66TU units.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II N74B66TU-A engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Early turbo oil feed line design - pre-2017 units more prone to coking; upgraded lines recommended during rebuild.
Valvetronic eccentric shaft sensor - intermittent power loss or limp mode on early-calibration N74B66TU-A engines.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II N74B66TU-B engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: High-pressure turbo components - Black Badge performance calibration increases thermal stress on turbo bearings.
Otto particle filter compatibility - 2017+ models require OPF-compliant rebuilds for ULEZ exemption.

engineGuide.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Rolls Royce Ghost Series II across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol
entries.history: The N74B66TU Technical Update V12 powers all Ghost Series II models, bringing refined twin-turbo performance and Goodwood engineering precision to the 2014-2020 facelift range.
compatibleVariants: Standard Wheelbase, Extended Wheelbase, Black Badge, Chauffeur Specification, London Specification
commonFailures: Turbo oil feed line coking - carbon buildup restricts oil flow to turbos, causing premature failure at 60k-90k miles
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases with blue smoke on high-mileage units after 70k miles
VANOS solenoid failure - variable valve timing faults cause rough idle and power loss on N74B66TU units
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66TU Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol
entries.history: Early-calibration N74B66TU-A variant fitted to 2014-2016 Ghost Series II models, featuring pre-2017 turbo oil feed design and initial Valvetronic mapping.
commonFailures: Early turbo oil feed line design - pre-2017 units more prone to coking; upgraded lines recommended during rebuild
Valvetronic eccentric shaft sensor - intermittent power loss or limp mode on early-calibration N74B66TU-A engines
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66TU-A Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine replacement

entries.title: 6.6L Petrol
entries.history: Late-calibration N74B66TU-B variant with revised ECU mapping and Otto particle filter compatibility, fitted to 2017-2020 Ghost Series II and all Black Badge models.
compatibleVariants: Standard Wheelbase (2017-2020), Extended Wheelbase (2017-2020), Black Badge (2016-2020)
commonFailures: High-pressure turbo components - Black Badge performance calibration increases thermal stress on turbo bearings
Otto particle filter compatibility - 2017+ models require OPF-compliant rebuilds for ULEZ exemption
entries.cta: Get quotes for N74B66TU-B Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Rolls Royce Ghost Series II's N74B66TU twin-turbo V12 is engineered for refined performance, but turbo oil feed coking, valve stem seal wear, and VANOS solenoid failures drive most replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Turbo Oil Feed Line Coking - Why N74B66TU Turbos Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Standard Wheelbase, Extended Wheelbase, Black Badge (2014-2020, N74B66TU, N74B66TU-A, N74B66TU-B)
problems.rootCause: Carbon deposits accumulate in narrow turbo oil feed lines during repeated short journeys, restricting lubrication flow and causing turbo bearing wear or seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Line Replacement - Replace oil feed lines before turbo failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, fit upgraded braided stainless steel oil feed lines with heat shielding
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents future turbo failure; recommended at 50k miles or during major service

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Rebuild - Rebuild or replace failed turbochargers
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, bench-test, and rebuild twin turbos with new bearings, seals, and wastegate actuators
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost performance; 30k-50k mile expectancy if oil feed issue resolved

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74B66TU Engine - Complete engine replacement with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N74B66TU with upgraded oil feed lines, tested turbos, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage Ghosts; addresses root cause and extends vehicle life

problems.recommendation: On a Rolls Royce Ghost Series II worth £80,000-£150,000, preventative oil feed line replacement at 50k miles offers exceptional value versus waiting for catastrophic turbo failure. If turbos have already failed, a rebuilt engine with upgraded lines is often more cost-effective than dealer turbo replacement.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N74B66TU engine prices for your Rolls Royce Ghost Series II

problems.h4: Valve Stem Seal Oil Consumption - Why Ghost Series II Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II all variants (2014-2020, N74B66TU family)
problems.rootCause: Original valve stem seals harden with age and heat cycles, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke on startup and increased oil consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Top-End Seal Replacement - Replace valve stem seals without full engine removal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace all 24 valve stem seals with updated Viton material, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for moderate oil consumption; 40k-60k mile improvement expected

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74B66TU Engine - Complete engine with updated seals throughout
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N74B66TU with upgraded seals, pistons, and rings; 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage Ghosts exceeding 100k miles; eliminates oil consumption permanently

problems.recommendation: If your Ghost Series II consumes over 1 litre per 1,000 miles with blue smoke, seal replacement is worthwhile on vehicles under 90k miles. Beyond that mileage, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Rolls Royce Ghost Series II valve seal repair or engine replacement

problems.group: VANOS Solenoid Variable Valve Timing Failure
problems.h4: VANOS Solenoid Variable Valve Timing Failure - Power Loss & Rough Idle
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II all variants (2014-2020, N74B66TU, N74B66TU-A)
problems.rootCause: VANOS solenoids controlling variable valve timing accumulate debris from oil breakdown, causing sluggish response, fault codes, and reduced performance or limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Solenoid Replacement - Replace faulty VANOS solenoids with updated parts
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose fault codes, replace intake/exhaust VANOS solenoids, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for early-stage issues; 30k-50k mile expectancy with regular oil changes

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing System Service - Full VANOS unit inspection and seal replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove VANOS units, replace seals, clean oil galleries, test variable timing function
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses underlying wear; recommended if solenoids fail repeatedly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74B66TU Engine - Complete engine with updated timing components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N74B66TU with upgraded VANOS components, tested timing system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for Ghosts with multiple timing-related faults or high mileage

problems.recommendation: For isolated VANOS faults under 70k miles, solenoid replacement is cost-effective. If multiple timing codes appear or the engine has exceeded 85k miles, a rebuilt engine prevents recurring issues and provides warranty peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare Rolls Royce Ghost Series II VANOS repair vs rebuilt engine costs

problems.group: Electronic Water Pump & Cooling System Failure
problems.h4: Electronic Water Pump & Cooling System Failure - Overheating Risk
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II all variants (2014-2020, N74B66TU family)
problems.rootCause: Electric water pump controllers fail due to heat exposure and electrical stress, causing intermittent cooling loss, overheating warnings, and potential engine damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Pump & Controller Replacement - Replace failed water pump assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain coolant, replace electric water pump and controller module, bleed cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Restores cooling function; 40k-60k mile expectancy with genuine parts

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74B66TU Engine - Complete engine if overheating caused damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt N74B66TU with upgraded cooling components, tested system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if overheating caused warping or head gasket failure

problems.recommendation: Address cooling warnings immediately on the N74B66TU V12. If no engine damage occurred, pump replacement is sufficient. If overheating persisted, a rebuilt engine prevents costly secondary failures.
problems.cta: Find Rolls Royce Ghost Series II cooling system repair or engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Rolls Royce Ghost Series II
finalCta.paragraph: The N74B66TU twin-turbo V12 is a masterpiece of engineering, but known failure patterns like turbo oil feed coking and valve stem seal wear can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Rolls Royce Ghost Series II specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these exact issues. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Rolls Royce Ghost Series II and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

years.preview: Ghost Series II Launch: N74B66TU Debut
years.description: The 2014 launch introduced the N74B66TU Technical Update V12, replacing the pre-facelift N74B66 with refined twin-turbo performance and updated Valvetronic mapping.

Revised turbo oil feed line design (later upgraded post-2016)
Standard and Extended Wheelbase variants launched simultaneously

Strong residual values support cost-effective rebuilt engine investment

faq.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Series II Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Rolls Royce Ghost Series II N74B66TU engine typically costs £15,000-£35,000 supply-only in the UK, with Black Badge and late-calibration variants at the higher end due to performance components and OPF compatibility requirements.
keyPoints: Standard Wheelbase rebuilt: £15,000-£32,000 typical range
Black Badge performance rebuilt: £18,000-£40,000 typical range
Supply & fit adds £3,500-£7,500 depending on specialist location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt N74B66TU engine prices for your Rolls Royce Ghost Series II

items.question: What engine codes fit my Rolls Royce Ghost Series II?
items.answer: All Rolls Royce Ghost Series II models (2014-2020) use the N74B66TU twin-turbo V12 family, with suffix variants N74B66TU-A (2014-2016) and N74B66TU-B (2017-2020, Black Badge) denoting calibration updates.
keyPoints: N74B66TU is the base code for all Ghost Series II variants
N74B66TU-A: early calibration, pre-upgraded turbo oil feed design
N74B66TU-B: late calibration, OPF-compatible, Black Badge performance mapping
Enter your registration above to verify exact code for your vehicle
items.cta: Verify your Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine code instantly

items.question: Is a reconditioned Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, when sourced from vetted UK specialists who compression-test, update known failure points like valve stem seals, and provide a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt N74B66TU engines.
keyPoints: Reconditioned N74B66TU engines undergo strip-down inspection and component renewal
Upgraded turbo oil feed lines prevent recurrence of coking failures
Compression and leak-down testing ensures factory-spec performance
Warranty terms vary; confirm unlimited mileage coverage before purchase
items.cta: Find reconditioned Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engines with warranty

items.question: What causes Rolls Royce Ghost Series II turbocharger failure?
items.answer: Turbo failure on the N74B66TU is most commonly caused by oil feed line coking, where carbon deposits restrict lubrication flow to turbo bearings, typically occurring between 60,000-90,000 miles on short-journey usage patterns.
keyPoints: Carbon buildup in narrow oil feed lines restricts turbo lubrication
Short journeys prevent oil reaching optimal temperature, accelerating coking
Upgraded braided stainless steel feed lines prevent recurrence during rebuild
Preventative line replacement at 50k miles avoids costly turbo failure
items.cta: Compare Rolls Royce Ghost Series II turbo repair vs rebuilt engine costs

items.question: Why does my Rolls Royce Ghost Series II consume excessive oil?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption on the N74B66TU is typically caused by hardened valve stem seals allowing oil into combustion chambers, producing blue smoke on startup and increasing consumption beyond 1 litre per 1,000 miles.
keyPoints: Original seals harden with age and heat cycles beyond 70k miles
Blue smoke on cold startup is the classic symptom of seal wear
Rebuilt engines include updated Viton seals addressing this failure point
Top-end seal replacement is viable under 90k miles; rebuilt engine preferred beyond
items.cta: Get quotes for Rolls Royce Ghost Series II valve seal or engine replacement

items.question: Can I use a used Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine from salvage?
items.answer: Yes, but verify compression test results, mileage history, and calibration suffix match before purchase; used N74B66TU engines offer budget options but carry higher risk than rebuilt units with warranty protection.
keyPoints: Request compression and leak-down test documentation before purchase
Confirm calibration suffix (A vs B) matches your vehicle's ECU requirements
Low-mileage donor engines from accident write-offs offer best value
Basic warranty typically 3 months; upgrade options may be available
items.cta: Check verified used Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine availability

items.question: Are Ghost Series I and Series II engines interchangeable?
items.answer: No, the Ghost Series II N74B66TU Technical Update engine is not directly interchangeable with the pre-facelift Series I N74B66 due to revised turbo calibration, Valvetronic mapping, and ECU compatibility requirements.
keyPoints: N74B66TU features updated turbo control strategy versus N74B66
ECU coding and immobiliser sync require exact calibration match
Physical mounting points are similar but electronics prevent cross-fitment
Always verify engine code via registration lookup before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify Rolls Royce Ghost Series II engine compatibility instantly

items.answer: A typical Rolls Royce Ghost Series II N74B66TU replacement takes 10-14 working days from quote acceptance to completion, including engine sourcing, specialist fitting, ECU coding, and road testing at an approved UK centre.

Specialist fitting: 5-7 days for N74B66TU V12 installation and coding
